Hey everybody, welcome to my web class. My name is Allison Prince. I'm so excited to have you guys here today. Now today what we're going to be covering is something that most people don't even think is possible and I'm guessing that you probably saw the headline before you got on here. Probably that's not really possible but you're curious, right? Like you want to find out what in the world this is even about, right? If that's true, this is the title. It's how my 15 year old sold 100,000, $270 and just nine short months and how you and your kids can actually do it too. How many of you guys would love to have your kids instead of like bugging you and begging you for money or actually producing money? How many of you guys want that for your kids? Okay. Now that's the first thing I'm going to be covering. Now what's interesting about this is the way the whole thing started is that my goal was never for me or for my kids to be like entrepreneurs. I do things entrepreneurially. I love having businesses and things but one day my daughter was sitting there and she wanted, she wasn't cleaning the house, she wasn't doing her homework. It's kind of frustrating to me. I said, look, you've got two options. One is you're going to move out and you're going to be gone out of my life. Number two, all right. Number two is you've got to do your homework and clean up your room. Number three, you've got to start a business. You can pick whichever one you want. Now my daughter is very smart apparently because she didn't move out and she didn't do her homework but what she did instead is she decided to actually go and start a business. But the problem is she didn't really know how to start a business and so I kind of do things about, you know, I've run a business so I totally gave her some guidelines and stuff but I wanted her to do it. I wanted her to do her success or to fail on her own and so I kind of gave her some basic guidelines and it kind of helped her in a point in the right direction and I let her run. And what was funny about it is for her to be able to start a business she did have to borrow some money so she borrowed some money from me and my husband and we actually lent it to her but with interest because I'm not going to, we don't give away free money. That's not something we do, right? So we gave her some money and she went out there and she went through this process. Now my daughter is pretty amazing but she's just like any other 15-year-old girl. She's not a 4.0 student. In fact, she got a 2.3 last semester which is kind of upsetting my husband and me but we still love her so she's not like a genius but she went out there and did this process and she went through the whole thing and it was crazy is that when she did the very first thing she went and she took the product and I'll show you guys the product here in a second what she did but she took the product and she charged selling it and her very first week after we launched it she made $5,000. Can you just imagine what it would be like to make $5,000 especially as a 15-year-old girl? It was insane and so I had this really cool teaching moment where I actually took my daughter and I took her inside and I had a pad of paper and I mapped this side and said you made $5,000. Now how much do you typically make as a babysitter? Which is well depending on if the people are cheaper, if they're rich, whatever it is but on average maybe $10 an hour and said okay for you two to make that same $5,000 babysitting, how many hours did I take in you? We did the math and it was 500 hours. Do you remember any diapers she had changed? How many late nights she had been up? How much, all these other things? It would have been hard but she did that the very first month after she got her product launch because she had this little business. Now that inspired her so much she started getting more and more excited. She actually went on this little pitch tank show and she had a chance to pitch her product and from that she made $1,500. So again, I used that as a teaching moment for my daughter. We brought it down, we sat down, I showed her and she realized to make that same amount of money she would have had a babysit, 150 kids over the period of time to make the same amount of money. She made from doing one pitch. And her next question for me was like, Mom, where else can I pitch this stuff? And that's the kind of thing that's really cool. And from that we had 5,000 people who clicked on the link to buy the, from the pillowcase. And from that 200 people actually bought the pillow, the pillowcase. Now I spent $1.13 to buy the pillowcase in China. It cost me 50 cents to get it shipped here to America. I paid the blogger $2 for every pillowcase that they sold. That means I made $12 for every single pillowcase that sold. We sold 200 of them. That was from one blogger. How many blogs are there around the world? There's a lot. Like not 10,000s and thousands of blogs. And that was from one person. So then I went to the next blogger and I did the same thing and I guess what happened. She sold 312 pillowcases.
